source: NFPA 101 Life Safety Code 2021 + OSHA 29 CFR 1910.36-1910.37 + IBC 2021 Chapter 10
topic: Emergency egress routes, exit door requirements, and travel distances for industrial occupancies

facts.context: NFPA 101 / IBC — max travel distance to exit in sprinklered industrial building (250 ft)
NFPA 101 — max travel distance to exit in non-sprinklered industrial building (200 ft)
NFPA 101 7.2.1.2 — 32 in minimum clear width for any exit door leaf
IBC — 36 in minimum for high-occupancy industrial spaces (50+ persons)
NFPA 101 — 44 in minimum corridor width serving 50 or more occupants
OSHA 1910.37(b) — 28 in minimum for low-occupancy exit routes
NFPA 101 — minimum 2 exits required for any occupant load above 1 person
IBC 1007.1.1 — exits must be separated by at least 1/3 of floor diagonal when unsprinklered
NFPA 101 — dead-end corridors must not exceed 20 ft in industrial occupancy
Clear floor space required on pull side of egress door — must not be blocked
NFPA 101 — exit signs required so no point is more than 100 ft from a visible sign
OSHA 1910.37(a) — exit routes must never be blocked by objects, equipment, or storage
NFPA 101 7.9 — emergency lighting must illuminate entire egress path at 44 in width
IBC 1011.2 — 44 in minimum stair width for industrial occupancy (multi-storey)
NFPA 101 — exit discharge to exterior must be at least as wide as the exit itself, min 60 in in high-hazard areas
